1.description: RBA purchases under different programs. This figure plots daily RBA purchases of Australian government bonds (AGBs) under yield curve control (YCC), market functioning (MF), and quantitative easing (QE). These three categories are defined in SectionI. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: Cumulative RBA purchases under long-dated OMOs and bond purchase programs. This figure plots cumulative RBA purchases of Australian government bonds (AGBs) under yield curve control (YCC), market functioning (MF), and quantitative easing (QE). The categories are defined in the SectionI. The left panel shows purchases in A$ and the right panel shows purchases as a share of total amount outstanding. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: Distribution of cumulative RBA purchases against amounts held by holders. This figure plots cumulative purchases of Australian government bonds (AGBs) as a share of amount outstanding under yield curve control (YCC), market functioning (MF), and quantitative easing (QE) as of December 31, 2020 and December 31, 2021, as well as those held by other investors. These three categories are defined in SectionI. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

4.description: Yields on AGBs targeted by YCC. The top panel of this figure shows the yield on AGBs targeted by the RBA's YCC program (Apr-23 and Apr-24) against the two securities maturing next, not targeted by the YCC program (Nov-24 and Apr-25). The YCC target switched from the Apr-23 to the Apr-24 AGB in October 2020 and is in effect between March 2020 and November 2021. The bottom panel shows the yield on AGBs targeted by YCC (Apr-23 issue prior to October 2020, and Apr-24 issue thereafter) against a duration-matched overnight index swap (OIS) rate and the expectations hypothesis component of this rate estimated from the affine term structure model. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

5.description: AGB yield curves on selected days. This figure plots yields on AGBs and highlights the securities included in the YCC program (Apr-23, and Apr-24) and the next-maturity security excluded from the YCC program (Nov-24). The figure also shows a Nelson-Siegel curve (details are in theInternet Appendix) estimated on AGB yields (zero-coupon AGB yield) and on OIS yields, as well as the YCC target yield level (YCC target) on each date. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

6.description: Forward rates on AGBs. This figure plots the bootstrapped forward AGB yield curve. Yields are bootstrapped under the assumption of a constant forward rate between maturity dates. The figure also includes zero-coupon (ZC) smoothed forward rates implied by a Nelson-Siegel curve estimated on AGBs. Details are in theInternet Appendix. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

7.description: Spreads of duration-matched state bond yields over select AGB rates. The figure plots the spread of each state's zero-coupon yield curve over the yield on the Apr-24 or Apr-26 AGB, where the duration of the state's yield curve is equal to the duration of the corresponding AGB. The thick lines are averages taken over spreads (thin lines) for New South Wales, Victoria, Queensland, Western Australia, and South Australia. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

8.description: Sensitivity of Australian and New Zealand OIS rate changes to U.S. counterparts. This figure plots the coefficient estimates from equation (1) (solid lines) along with 90% confidence intervals based on robust standard errors for both countries for select sample periods. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

9.description: Impact of YCC on the swap and government bond yield curves in the calibrated model. This figure plots the bond and swap yield curves implied by the model of SectionIII. The YCC target is always set to 10 bps but the mean (μi$\mu _i$) and standard deviation (σi$\sigma _i$) of period-2 and -3 short-term rates differ across the three calibrations as summarized in TableV. Additional parameters used in the model calibration are discussed in SectionIII. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
